name: Plate sightings track on the route detail timeline
summary: Surface plate sightings on the existing route detail page as a new track on the signal timeline. Clicking a sighting marker seeks the video; hovering shows a thumbnail and the detected plate + vehicle badge. Visible only when alpr_enabled is true (frontend reads /v1/settings/alpr). Severity-colored: gray for unalerted, amber for sev 2-3, red for sev 4-5. Don't fork the existing SignalTimeline component; add a sibling track inside it. Existing component lives at web/src/components/video/SignalTimeline.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: New hook web/src/lib/useAlprSettings.ts: fetches /v1/settings/alpr once on mount, exposes {enabled, engineReachable, ...}. Caches via SWR or the project's existing API client cache. Default state during loading: do NOT reserve UI space (avoid flicker).

New component web/src/components/video/PlateTimeline.tsx. Sits inside the existing signal timeline rail (find the SignalTimeline render and add the new track immediately below the engagement track or wherever fits the existing visual hierarchy).

Each encounter renders as a horizontal segment from first_seen_ts to last_seen_ts on the timeline's time axis. Color-coded by severity: gray (severity 0/null), amber (2-3), red (4-5). Sev 1 not used by the heuristic but treat as gray.

Hover tooltip: plate text, vehicle badge (color dot in the literal vehicle color when known + 'Make Model' label when signature present), 'Followed through N turns' when turn_count >= 1, ack status, click-to-seek hint.
Click jumps the player currentTime to first_seen_ts (use the same seek API SignalTimeline already uses for engagement events).

Severity > 0 segments get a small icon badge (alert-triangle) that is ALSO visible in the minimap/scrubber at the bottom of the player so the user can spot alerted encounters without scrolling the timeline.
Data fetching: single call to /v1/routes/{dongle}/{route}/plates via the existing apiFetch helper. Refetch on route change only.
Empty state: zero encounters -> render nothing (do not reserve a blank rail with a placeholder; visual noise is worse than absence).
Feature flag check: useAlprSettings().enabled === true gates the entire PlateTimeline render. When false, render returns null.
Lint clean (pnpm --dir web lint), type-check clean (pnpm --dir web type-check), tests added for the empty state, feature-flag-off state, and click-to-seek behavior.

Manual verification on a route with >=3 encounters of varying severity: load the route, verify color coding matches the encounter severity, hover shows correct vehicle badge, click seeks the video, alerted markers visible in scrubber.
